description: Fix login broken (no error shown), downgrade vite to ^5.4.19, fix Settings cert tab for panel HTTPS, check bot startup, redesign proxy config to simple HTTP/SOCKS5 for node-panel API connection, remove redundant L2TP fields, fix SSH unknown status, add SSH accounting, simplify core settings UI
steps: Step 1 - Fix vite version: In panel/web/admin/package.json and panel/web/portal/package.json, change vite from '^6.0.0' to '^5.4.19'. This fixes ERESOLVE npm error on deploy.

Step 2 - Fix admin login (no error shown): In panel/web/admin/src/stores/auth.ts, the login() method catches all errors silently and returns false. The useApi composable sets error.value before throwing, but the error might get cleared by the preflight health check. Fix: (a) Remove the preflight GET /api/health from login() since CSRF is exempt for /api/auth/, (b) Make the catch block explicitly set a user-friendly error message like 'Invalid username or password' when the API returns 401, and (c) Ensure the error ref value persists after the catch so LoginView can display it. Also verify LoginView shows store.error correctly.

Step 3 - Fix Settings cert tab: In panel/web/admin/src/views/SettingsView.vue, the certificates tab currently shows CA and TLS-Crypt status which is for OpenVPN. Change it to be about panel HTTPS certificates (SSL cert + key for the panel's own HTTPS). Replace the OpenVPN cert display with panel HTTPS certificate management (upload cert/key, show expiry, enable/disable HTTPS). For OpenVPN certs, they should be auto-generated or uploaded per-node in the cores config section.

Step 4 - Fix bot startup: In panel/cmd/panel/main.go, the bot reads config from environment variables (PANEL_TG_BOT_TOKEN, PANEL_TG_ENABLED, PANEL_TG_CHAT_ID). But the admin UI saves telegram settings to panel_settings DB table (telegram_token, telegram_chat_id). The bot is initialized BEFORE it can read DB settings. Fix: After DB init and migration, read telegram settings from the panel_settings table and use those values (with env vars as override/fallback). Also add a re-init mechanism so saving settings in UI restarts the bot.

Step 5 - Redesign proxy config in NodesView: The current 'outbound' config in each protocol's extra_json is designed for VPN traffic routing (xray/vless/vmess). This is WRONG. 'Proxy' means a simple HTTP or SOCKS5 proxy setting for the node agent's HTTP client to reach the panel API (like Pasargad censorship bypass). Remove the per-protocol outbound config from PROTOCOL_DEFAULTS and the Proxy Group form section. Replace with a single node-level 'API Proxy' configuration section in the nodes tab (not per-protocol) with fields: enabled (bool), type (HTTP/SOCKS5), address (host:port), username (optional), password (optional). This goes in the node edit form or a separate proxy config per node.

Step 6 - Remove redundant L2TP fields: In panel/web/admin/src/views/NodesView.vue, remove the 'Refuse CHAP', 'Refuse PAP', and 'Require MS-CHAPv2' toggle fields from the L2TP security group. Keep only the 'Auth Method' dropdown (CHAP/PAP/MS-CHAPv2). Also remove refuse_chap, refuse_pap, require_mschap_v2 from L2TP PROTOCOL_DEFAULTS extra_json.

Step 7 - Fix SSH 'unknown' status: The getServiceStatus() function in NodesView.vue checks node.services array first then falls back to status_metrics. The issue is the node_status table stores openvpn_status, l2tp_status, ikev2_status but NOT ssh_status. SSH is in node_services table. Fix: In panel/internal/api/api.go, add ssh_status field to NodeStatus struct and the node_status INSERT/UPDATE. In the node push handler, extract SSH status from services map and store it alongside other statuses. Update the frontend to also check metrics.ssh_status.

Step 8 - SSH accounting enhancement: Add traffic tracking fields to the SSH protocol config. In the PROTOCOL_DEFAULTS for SSH extra_json, add: accounting_enabled (bool, default true), accounting_interval (number, seconds, default 300). These tell the node agent to periodically report SSH session traffic to RADIUS accounting. Add corresponding form fields in the SSH section.

Step 9 - Simplify core settings UI: The cores tab shows too many fields at once. Implement a collapsible/accordion approach: Show only 'Essential' fields by default (port, network, enabled, auth method). Put advanced fields (MTU, max_clients, keepalive, verbosity, cipher details, DNS, etc.) behind an 'Advanced Settings' toggle/expandable section. This applies to all protocols.

Step 10 - Update i18n: Add any new translation keys needed for the changes above to all 4 locale files (en.ts, fa.ts, zh.ts, ru.ts). Keys for: proxy config labels, SSH accounting, simplified UI labels, panel HTTPS cert labels.
acceptance_criteria: vite version is '^5.4.19' in both admin and portal package.json
Login shows 'Invalid username or password' error when credentials are wrong
Login succeeds and navigates to dashboard when credentials are correct
Settings cert tab shows panel HTTPS certificate management instead of OpenVPN certs
Bot reads token from panel_settings DB table if env vars are not set
Proxy config in nodes is a simple per-node HTTP/SOCKS5 config for API connection, not per-protocol xray outbound
L2TP config only shows auth_method dropdown, no refuse_chap/refuse_pap/require_mschapv2 toggles
SSH shows proper status from node_services (not unknown when service is running)
SSH config has accounting_enabled and accounting_interval fields
Core settings show essential fields by default with advanced toggle
All new keys translated in EN/FA/ZH/RU
npm run build succeeds in both admin and portal
verification: cd panel/web/admin && npm install && npm run build
cd panel/web/portal && npm install && npm run build
findings: All 10 steps completed and verified. Admin and portal build with vite 5.4.21. Go build and all tests pass. The login fix removes the unnecessary preflight health check and explicitly shows error messages on 401. Proxy is now a per-node API proxy (HTTP/SOCKS5) instead of per-protocol xray outbound. L2TP only shows auth_method dropdown. SSH gets accounting fields and proper status from node_services. Advanced settings hidden by default behind toggle.
